titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Elder to the Under Secretary, 30 June, 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Elder

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Robert Elder to the Undersecretary. Elder writes to apply for the position of Clerk of Crown and Peace for Co. Westmeath. In this letter he outlines his professional experience which includes his appointment to Commissioner of the Oaths in Westmeath.Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 - 1938) was sent to Ireland as replacement under-secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5615/11170

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ROBERT ELDER, Solicitor. Dublin Office, 69 & 70 Dame Street. Telegrams: 'Elder, Athlone.' Telephone No 9. Notary Public. Commr for Oaths. 30 June 1916 Dear Sir, I beg to apply for the vacant Clerk of the Crown and
Peaceship for the County of Westmeath. I was admitted a Solicitor
in 1903 and have since practised at Athlone and I have recently
been appointed by the Lord Chancellor a Notary Public and a
Commissioner for Oaths for the County of Westmeath. I should
feel obliged therefore if you would kindly bring my application
under the notice of the proper authority. I am,
Your obedient servant, Robert Elder The Under Secretary, The Castle, Dublin.
